response:
This code implements the Union-Find (Disjoint Set Union) data structure for managing disjoint sets of n elements, returning two functions: `find(x)` which returns the root representative of the set containing x (using path compression for efficiency) and `union(x, y)` which merges the sets containing x and y (using union by rank to minimize tree height).

The algorithm employs path compression in `find` to flatten the tree during root traversal and union by rank to attach the shorter tree to the taller one, achieving amortized O(α(n)) time complexity per operation, where α is the inverse Ackermann function (nearly constant).
